install_tell: docker run -p 8000:8000 ragapp/ragapp  (or `docker compose up` in deployments/single bundling qdrant+ollama)
misconfig_patterns: Operator runs `docker run -p 8000:8000 ragapp/ragapp` and binds it to a public interface without the API-gateway auth the README tells them to add -> /admin and /api/management fully open
Publishing the bundled compose's Qdrant (6333) and Ollama (11434) ports to 0.0.0.0 alongside 8000 -> unauth vector store + unauth local LLM
Configuring OPENAI_API_KEY / LLAMA_CLOUD_API_KEY in the container then exposing the open /api/management/config write surface -> attacker repoints model provider, abuses paid keys (llmjacking)
Treating 'deployable in your own cloud like a custom GPT' marketing as 'secure by default' - the auth gap is the operator's responsibility and is silently absent

active_probe.false_positive_check: RAGapp is built on create-llama scaffolding, so the generic chat routes (/api/chat, /api/files) collide with stock create-llama / LlamaIndex chat apps and with thousands of unrelated FastAPI hosts (any FastAPI app serves /docs and /openapi.json). To avoid the ~50%-rule false positive, anchor on the RAGapp-UNIQUE management surface: (1) the `/api/management/config`, `/api/management/agents`, `/api/management/reranker`, `/api/management/llamacloud` route family is RAGapp-specific (plain create-llama has no /api/management); (2) the literal admin app at `/admin` serving a Next.js RAGapp builder UI plus the string "RAGapp" in the HTML; (3) presence of /api/management AND /api/chat AND /admin together. A host that has /api/chat but no /api/management is upstream create-llama, NOT RAGapp - do not tag it. Confirm by reading the OpenAPI at /openapi.json for the /api/management/* path group.

deployment_tells: Next.js admin builder served at /admin with the brand string 'RAGapp' and a 'Congratulations 🎉' first-run dialog
FastAPI /docs (Swagger) and /openapi.json enumerating the /api/management/* and /api/chat/* router groups together
Default model on the bundled compose is phi3:latest via Ollama (MODEL=${MODEL:-phi3:latest}); standalone .env defaults MODEL_PROVIDER=openai, MODEL=gpt-4o-mini
Co-hosted Qdrant (6333/6334) and Ollama (11434) on the same IP from the single-deployment compose = the RAGapp+Qdrant+Ollama bundle
ragapp/ragapp Docker image; APP_HOST 0.0.0.0 APP_PORT 8000; static file mounts at /api/files/data and /api/files/output
pivot_paths: GET /openapi.json or /docs -> enumerate full route map and confirm /api/management group (read-only fingerprint confirmation)
GET /api/management/config/models -> read configured model provider + whether an API key is set (is_configured), no key value exfil needed - presence is the finding
GET /api/management/agents -> read agent definitions, system prompt, tools (business-logic disclosure, metadata only)
GET /api/management/files -> enumerate ingested document filenames (names ARE the finding; do not download corpus)
If 6333 also exposed: GET qdrant /collections -> list vector collections (metadata) confirming co-located unauth vector store
GET /api/chat/config -> read chat UI config / starter questions revealing the deployment's purpose
vulnerabilities: No CVE or GHSA assigned as of 2026-06-12 (github.com/ragapp/ragapp/security/advisories: 'There aren't any published security advisories'). The exposure is a documented design choice (no built-in auth), not a tracked vulnerability - it is a misconfiguration/insecure-default class, not a patchable CVE.

Design-level: /api/management/* (read+write config, files, agents) and /api/chat ship with zero authentication; the only control is request_limit_middleware (rate limiting). Affects all versions/tags (the architecture has had no in-container auth since initial release).
Bundled Qdrant in deployments/single/docker-compose.yml runs with QDRANT_API_KEY="" (no vector-DB auth) - inherited exposure if 6333 is published alongside 8000.
auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE, three corroborating files at github.com/ragapp/ragapp (main). (1) README.md states verbatim: "Just the RAGapp container doesn't come with any authentication layer by design. This is the task of an API Gateway routing the traffic to RAGapp." The README's own quickstart is `docker run -p 8000:8000 ragapp/ragapp` and exposes Admin UI /admin, Chat UI /, API /docs with no login step. (2) src/ragapp/main.py mounts the management router at prefix `/api/management` with NO auth dependency; the only middleware anywhere is `Depends(request_limit_middleware)` on the chat and upload routers (a rate limiter, not authentication). (3) src/ragapp/backend/routers/management/config.py defines GET/POST `/chat`, `/models`, `/is_configured`, `/models/list` with no auth dependency on any route. The default deployments/single/docker-compose.yml sets no auth variable for the ragapp service and explicitly `QDRANT_API_KEY=""`. Result: running the documented quickstart and exposing :8000 yields a fully unauthenticated admin UI AND REST API. The vendor explicitly off-loads auth to an external API gateway the operator must add; nothing in the container ships it.
sensitive_assets: Upstream LLM provider API keys / config: /api/management/config returns model_provider settings; the container holds OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI/Anthropic, and LLAMA_CLOUD_API_KEY (config/.env) - write access via POST /api/management/config/models lets an attacker reconfigure the model provider and read/redirect key usage

Ingested RAG documents / source knowledge base: GET /api/management/files lists and POST uploads/deletes the indexed corpus; static mounts /api/files/data and /api/files/output serve the raw uploaded files directly - any PII/proprietary docs an operator indexed are readable

Agent / flow definitions revealing business logic: /api/management/agents exposes the configured agent name, role, backstory, tools, and the SYSTEM_PROMPT (config/agents.yaml + config/.env) - the full custom-GPT definition

Raw vector store contents: the bundled compose exposes Qdrant on 6333/6334 (and Chroma when configured) on the same host with QDRANT_API_KEY="", so embeddings/chunks are directly readable if that port is also exposed
Unauthenticated LLM inference / cost abuse: POST /api/chat is open, letting anyone run the operator's paid model (llmjacking) and exfiltrate the RAG corpus via crafted queries
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
